Welcome to this first edition of Frontiers in Brief - our new look newsletter with a wealth of interesting hot picks – this month focusing on the Open Science movement. Read a fascinating piece on the Venice Time Machine project, learn more about Microsoft Academic as a platform to navigate this century’s deluge of new science or submit an idea for a Frontiers Research Topic to make an impact in the world - from saving the Arctic to overcoming global obesity, we all need Open Science. Finally, we need your help. Please tell us what you think of our new Frontiers in Brief by taking our short survey. We are excited to be at the forefront of Open Science and know that we can continue to be so with your support – our community of researchers who believe in the same
 thing. Thank you!


Your Frontiers Team

Can 10-million wind turbines save the Arctic?

Climate change is perhaps one of the greatest threats to humanity. In events that have baffled scientists, this northern winter has seen such record low levels of sea ice that there have been proposals to re-freeze the Arctic with ten million wind powered pumps over the ice cap. For almost ten years Frontiers has been at the forefront of Open Science making important, high-quality research freely available to catalyze solutions to our 21st century problems. Do you have a Research Topic idea that will help to make our planet a better place?

Welcome to this first edition of Frontiers in Brief - our new look newsletter with a wealth of interesting hot picks – this month focusing on the Open Science movement. Read a fascinating piece on the Venice Time Machine project, learn more about Microsoft Academic  as a platform to navigate this century’s deluge of new science or submit an idea for a Frontiers Research Topic to make an impact in the world - from saving the Arctic to overcoming global obesity, we all need Open Science. Finally, we need your help. Please tell us what you think of our new Frontiers in Brief by taking our short survey. We are excited to be at the forefront of Open Science and know that we can continue to be so with your support – our community of researchers who believe in the same thing. Thank you!

Your Frontiers Team
